items.description: Arabic numerals 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 written in the right margin, aligned to text lines. Modern hand distinct from the main insular/Gothic bookhand.
items.scholarly_note: Later editorial line numbering; not original to the 13th-century codex.

items.description: Small dark mark resembling the numeral '6' positioned above the first line of text.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly modern foliation, quire mark, or annotation; requires comparison with other folios.

items.description: Dark brown irregular stain on parchment, extending slightly into the right edge of the text column.
items.scholarly_note: Consistent with moisture damage, handling, or age-related discoloration.

items.region: lower half of page, lines 18-30 and below text block
items.description: Broad brownish discoloration and diffuse staining across the lower portion of the folio.
items.scholarly_note: Typical foxing and oxidation of medieval vellum.

items.description: Multiple white linear scratches and surface abrasions running horizontally and diagonally across several lines of text, with localized ink and surface loss.
items.scholarly_note: Mechanical wear from rubbing or scraping.

items.region: bottom third and bottom right corner
items.description: Severe abrasion with large pale/white patches where parchment surface and ink have been worn away. Bottom right corner is frayed with irregular edge and material loss.
items.scholarly_note: Heavy use or environmental damage; text in this zone is faded but partially legible.

items.description: Irregular, possibly trimmed or torn top edge with minor parchment loss at the upper right of the written area.

items.region: scattered across page, especially right and lower areas
items.description: Small dark spots and general yellow-brown age discoloration of the vellum.
items.scholarly_note: Possible flyspecks, mold residue, or oxidation; common in this manuscript.

scratches: Multiple horizontal white abrasions crossing lines ~23-29 in lower text block
Fine diagonal and linear scratches with ink loss in bottom right text area
damage_notes: Extensive surface abrasion and material loss in lower third, worst at bottom right corner
Irregular top edge with minor parchment loss
Overall vellum thinning, edge fraying, and localized ink fading from wear
summary: Page 15 shows a single column of medieval script with no original marginalia, doodles, pen trials, or visible scribal corrections. Modern line numbers occupy the right margin. Primary features are physical damage: heavy abrasion and white scratches in the lower third, multiple brown stains, and edge wear at top and bottom. Text remains mostly legible despite wear.
